title: 1.1: Tests that defrag() returns three tuple lists (nonfrag, unfrag, badfrag) when provided with a list of fragmented packets.
1.2: Tests that defragment() returns a single reconstructed packet when provided with a list of IP fragments in correct order.
1.3: Tests that defragment() and defrag() both return packets with the timestamp of the last fragment when fragments arrive with different timestamps.
1.4: Tests that defragment() returns a single defragmented packet when the first fragment is missing but other fragments are present.
1.5: Tests that defragment() and defrag() both correctly handle real-world fragmented DNS response packets by reconstructing the complete DNS packet with valid DNSRR.
2.1: Places the test for verifying defrag() sorting functionality in test/scapy/layers/inet.uts.
2.2: Places the test for verifying defragment() reconstruction functionality in test/scapy/layers/inet.uts.
2.3: Places the test for verifying timestamp handling in defragment() and defrag() in test/scapy/layers/inet.uts.
2.4: Places the test for verifying missing fragment handling in defragment() in test/scapy/layers/inet.uts.
2.5: Places the test for verifying real DNS packet fragmentation in defrag() and defragment() in test/scapy/layers/inet.uts.
3.1: All tests use framework UTscapy with assert statements for validation.
3.2: All tests follow the test naming pattern `= function_name() <optional_description>`.
